38: **Spain**, level with the rest of **Group H** on a single point after their opening draw, face **Saudi Arabia**, who sit in exactly the same position — all four sides yet to be separated. A European giant against a Gulf state that knows how to frustrate: expect Spain to dominate possession but Saudi Arabia to make them work hard for every inch of space.
39: **Belgium** and **IR Iran** meet in **Group G** with both sides level on one point among all four teams, making this a pivotal chance to break clear of the logjam. The Red Devils carry the weight of expectation but Iran's disciplined defensive shape can absorb pressure and punish on the counter — a draw serves neither side, and both know it.
37: **Uruguay** and **Cabo Verde** are both level with the rest of **Group H** on a single point, with positions entirely unseparated heading into this 3:00 PM PT clash. La Celeste's rugged defensive resilience meets a Cabo Verde side that proved against the draw they can hold their own — but Uruguay's greater big-tournament pedigree should ultimately tell.
40: Closing out the day at 6:00 PM PT, **New Zealand** and **Egypt** are both among all four **Group G** teams level on one point, with no side yet separated. The All Whites will look to exploit set-pieces and physicality, while Egypt's technical midfield could control the tempo — with **Belgium** vs **IR Iran** already in the books by kick-off, both sides will know exactly what result they need.
34: **Ecuador** are level with **Curaçao** in **Group E** — both sides on 0 points after one game, though Ecuador's goal difference of -1 already edges them ahead of Curaçao's -6 following their heavy opening defeat. La Tri's miserly defensive structure should prove too much for the tournament's smallest-ever qualifier, but with only one shot at three points in this fixture, Ecuador cannot afford complacency.
36: **Japan** sit among three teams in **Group F** on one point after their opening draw, while **Tunisia** are fourth on 0 points and already up against it after a 4-0 defeat — making this a must-win for the Carthage Eagles. The Samurai Blue's quick, technical passing game will test Tunisia's defensive discipline, but after **Netherlands**' five-goal hammering of **Sweden** earlier today, Japan know a win here would put them right back in contention for the knockouts.
